title: Cogswellia eastwoodiae
taxonomy: Cogswellia eastwoodiae is a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. It belongs to the genus Cogswellia, which is a monotypic genus. This means that Cogswellia eastwoodiae is the only species within the genus. Further classification places it in the order Brassicales and the clade core eudicots. The plant is endemic to a specific region, adding to its unique classification within the botanical world.

intro: Cogswellia eastwoodiae, also known as Eastwood's Cogswellia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Cistaceae that is endemic to the Philippines. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in the treatment of various health conditions such as wounds, fever, and respiratory infections. Bioactive constituents of Cogswellia eastwoodiae include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
